questions.text: Two triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are:
questions.explanation: Two triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are equal and corresponding sides are in the same ratio.

questions.text: In a right triangle, the square of hypotenuse equals sum of squares of other two sides.
questions.explanation: This is the Pythagoras theorem: In a right triangle, hypotenuse² = base² + height²

questions.text: What is the AAA similarity criterion?
questions.correctAnswer: If corresponding angles of two triangles are equal, the triangles are similar.
questions.explanation: AAA (Angle-Angle-Angle) similarity: If two triangles have all corresponding angles equal, they are similar.

questions.text: The ratio of areas of two similar triangles is equal to the square of the ratio of their corresponding ________.
questions.explanation: For similar triangles, Area₁/Area₂ = (side₁/side₂)². The ratio of areas equals the square of the ratio of corresponding sides.
